analysis.problem: Lack of regulatory framework for asteroid mining and space resource utilization
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient international cooperation and lack of clear laws and regulations governing space resource extraction
leverage_points: Government agencies and international organizations, such as the United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space
Private companies involved in asteroid mining and space resource utilization, such as Planetary Resources and Deep Space Industries

proposed_actions.action: Establish a clear regulatory framework for asteroid mining and space resource utilization
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Develop and implement international laws and regulations within the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Increase investment in research and development of sustainable space resource utilization technologies
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Allocate at least $100 million in funding for research and development within the next 2 years

evidence: The Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015 (H.R. 1508) was introduced but never became law
The European Space Resources Innovation Centre (ESRIC) is a research, business, and innovation hub dedicated to space resource utilization
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Earth (sustainable resource utilization) and Civilization (international cooperation and regulation)
analysis.summary: The lack of a regulatory framework for asteroid mining and space resource utilization poses a medium-level risk to the sustainable development of space resources, and addressing this issue requires international cooperation and investment in research and development.

raw_items.title: Asteroid mining
raw_items.summary: Asteroid mining is the hypothetical and technically possible extraction of materials from asteroids and other minor planets, including near-Earth objects.Research missions focused on asteroid sample return, including Hayabusa, Hayabusa2, OSIRIS-REx, and Tianwen-2, illustrate the challenges of collecting ore from space using current technology. raw_items.title: Asteroid Mining
raw_items.summary: The asteroid belt in our solar system contains ~8% metal-rich (M type) asteroids and 75% volatile-rich carbonaceous (C type) asteroids ("Asteroid belt", 2010). raw_items.title: Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015
raw_items.summary: The Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015 (H.R. 1508) was a bill (which never became law) introduced in the 114th Congress of the U.S. on March 19, 2015, by Rep. Bill Posey, Republican of Florida. It is identical to the Senate bill by the same name (S. 976).
